family: Fries-, Photo-Fries, and Anionic Ortho-Fries Rearrangement
rationale: The Fries rearrangement of phenyl acetate to 2-hydroxyacetophenone is a classic, textbook example of this reaction family. It clearly demonstrates the migration of an acyl group from a phenolic oxygen to an ortho position on the aromatic ring, catalyzed by a Lewis acid (AlCl3). The reactants and products are simple, acyclic where possible, and unambiguously represent the core transformation.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses the simplest possible aromatic system (phenyl) and acyl group (acetyl) to avoid overlap with more complex named reactions involving substituted arenes or specific functional groups. It avoids motifs like diazo groups, ring expansions, or radical initiators that could cause collisions with other reaction families.
